lanes.objective: Extract a reusable product-family kernel from the closed homepage and /imc laws, and separate flagship-only behavior from generic dossier law.
acceptance: kernel law artifact exists
generic /work/[slug] law is explicit
flagship exceptions are separated from family law

lanes.objective: Verify that XR and FT packets satisfy the minimum binding requirements for generic product routes without presentation-time fabrication.
acceptance: packet bindings remain honest
fallback rules are explicit

lanes.objective: Define and validate the falsification bundle for the product-family kernel before rollout starts.
acceptance: kernel QA bundle is declared
generic route promotion rules are explicit

lanes.objective: Audit the XR packet and GitHub truth surfaces that feed the first generic proof page.
acceptance: XR packet truth is sufficient and current

lanes.objective: Apply the locked product-family kernel to /work/xr without bespoke route hacks.
acceptance: XR renders from the kernel
XR remains packet-truthful

lanes.objective: Run falsification against /work/xr and reject any kernel or responsive regression.
acceptance: XR has zero critical and zero major diffs

lanes.objective: Replay the accepted XR candidate through the integration lane before promotion.
acceptance: XR candidate replays cleanly in integration

lanes.objective: Audit FT packet sufficiency and route bindings before replication promotion.
acceptance: FT packet truth is sufficient and current

lanes.objective: Prove the same kernel generalizes to /work/ft without undeclared bespoke logic.
acceptance: FT is produced from the same kernel
replication delta is explainable by content or declared variants

lanes.objective: Run falsification against /work/ft and the kernel inheritance boundary.
acceptance: FT has zero critical and zero major diffs

lanes.objective: Replay the accepted FT candidate through the integration lane before promotion.
acceptance: FT candidate replays cleanly in integration

lanes.objective: Verify that the recursive machine is operating on stable laws rather than moving targets.
acceptance: automation would operate on stable laws

lanes.objective: Confirm that guarded recursion rejects regressions and respects locks, paths, and overflow rules.
acceptance: safety gates remain intact

lanes.objective: Replay all accepted canonical and generic routes through integration before any unattended cadence begins.
acceptance: integration replay is current
